texture Element

The <texture> element specifies a standard 2D texture.

<texture
   id = string
   height = integer
   width = integer
   levels = integer
   source = string
   alphaSource = string
   alphaAmount = integer
   colorKey = string
   filter = string
   format = string
   mapData = string
   bumpAmount = integer
/>

Required Attributes

id

Texture identifier used for referencing from a <textureState> element. For more details, see XDX id Attribute.

source

Source image file. For more details, see Supported Image File Types. This attribute is not required if a mipmap level 0 is explictly included in the texture using a <mip> element. If there is an alpha channel in the source bitmap, that alpha channel is preserved and output. If there is an alpha channel in the source bitmap and the alphaSource attribute is specified, the alphaSource bitmap will replace the alpha channel given in the source bitmap.

Optional Attributes

height

Height in texels of the output texture. Must be between 1 and 4096. If this attribute is not included, the source texture height is used. If this attribute is specified and the source image height does not match, the compiler will use a triangular filter with no dithering to scale the image.

width

Width in texels of the output texture. Must be between 1 and 4096. If this attribute is not included, the source texture width is used. If this attribute is specified and the source image width does not match, the compiler will use a triangular filter with no dithering to scale the image.

levels

Number of mipmap levels to automatically generate. Must be between 0 and 13. To generate mipmaps all the way down to 1×1, use 0. This attribute should not be specified if the <texture> tag contains <mip> elements. For RGB textures, mipmaps are generated using a triangular filter with dithering. For UVL textures, mipmaps are generated using a point filter with dithering. The filtering used to generate the mipmaps cannot be changed using the XDX file.

alphaSource

Alpha channel source image file. For more details, see Supported Image File Types. The alpha channel is computed by converting the alphaSource image to a full 32-bit color image and taking the average of the red, blue, and green channels as the alpha channel.

alphaAmount

Controls the transparency of the alpha channel for the texture. If specified, this attribute must be between 0 and 100. The default value is 100. This attribute is treated as a percentage and is used to clamp alpha vlaues. For example, if alphaAmount is 25, the clamp value will be 63 (255×0.25) and all alpha channel values less than 63 will be clamped to 63.

colorKey

Used to specify the color key the compiler should use to determine what colors to convert to alpha. By default, the color key alpha component is zero and no color key operations are performed.

filter

Specifies the filter used to resize the image.

If this value is specified, it must contain one of the following flags:

Value Description
D3DX_FILTER_NONE No scaling or filtering will take place. Pixels outside the bounds of the source image are assumed to be transparent black.
D3DX_FILTER_POINT Each destination pixel is computed by sampling the nearest pixel from the source image.
D3DX_FILTER_TRIANGLE Every pixel in the source image contributes equally to the destination image. This is the slowest of the filters.
D3DX_FILTER_LINEAR Each destination pixel is computed by sampling the four nearest pixels from the source image. This filter works best when the scale on both axes is less than two.
D3DX_FILTER_BOX Each pixel is computed by averaging a 2×2(×2) box of pixels from the source image. This filter works only when the dimensions of the destination are half those of the source, as is the case with mipmaps.

In addition, zero or more of the following flags may be ORed in with one of the flags above.

Value Description
D3DX_FILTER_WRAP Pixels will be wrapped, not mirrored. This is the default behavior.
D3DX_FILTER_MIRROR Specifying this flag is the same as specifying the D3DX_FILTER_MIRROR_U, D3DX_FILTER_MIRROR_V, and D3DX_FILTER_MIRROR_W flags.
D3DX_FILTER_MIRROR_U Pixels off the edge of the texture on the u-axis should be mirrored, not wrapped.
D3DX_FILTER_MIRROR_V Pixels off the edge of the texture on the v-axis should be mirrored, not wrapped.
D3DX_FILTER_MIRROR_W Pixels off the edge of the texture on the w-axis should be mirrored, not wrapped.
D3DX_FILTER_DITHER This flag indicates that the resulting image be dithered using a 4×4 ordered dither algorithm.
format

Format for the output texture. Must be a member of the D3DFORMAT enumeration; however, it cannot be a depth/stencil format. If the output format is not specified, it will default to D3DFMT_A8R8G8B8.

mapData

If this attribute is defined, it must be one of the following values:

Value Description
NormalMap Specifies that the texture is a normal map.
Generate NormalMap Specifies that the texture should be converted to a normal map from a height map.
bumpAmount

Scales the height field making a normal map bumpier. This attribute is used during the conversion of the texture from a height map to a normal map (when mapData is "Generate NormalMap"). The attribute can range from 0 to 100—the larger the number, the bumpier the normal map. The default value is 100.

Element Information

Parent Elements XDX
Child Elements mip

Remarks

To specify a texture directly for use in mipmapping, use the <mip> element and not the <texture> element.

The source file does not need to have the size or format specified in the height, width, and format attributes. The texture will be resized and the format converted to match the attributes.

Examples

A. No mipmapping

<texture id="Forest" source="textures/forest.bmp" format="D3DFMT_LIN_R5G6B5" />

B. Automatically generating mipmapping levels

Setting levels to zero will generate all mipmapping levels down to 1×1.

<texture id="greydirt" source="textures/grydirt1.bmp" width="256" height="128" levels="0" />
C. Using filters with mipmapping

<texture id="lightoak" source="textures/LightOaksm.bmp" 
         format="D3DFMT_DXT1" levels="0" 
         filter="D3DX_FILTER_TRIANGLE | D3DX_FILTER_MIRROR" />

D. Directly specifying mipmaps

<texture id="DetailTexture" width="256" height="256" format="D3DFMT_DXT4" > 
   <mip index="0" source="textures/detail0.bmp" > 
   <mip index="1" source="textures/detail1.bmp" > 
   <mip index="2" source="textures/detail2.bmp" > 
</texture>

See Also

<mip>
